game_name: The Legend of the Mystical Ninja

danger_signals.visual_form: Ghost Woman at temple, ghost animals emerging from temple at night
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat, corrupted sacred space, need for investigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Hyotoko dancers (suspicious townspeople reactions), Otafu Army members with mime/clown appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Infiltration of towns, disguised threat, loss of control over settlements

danger_signals.visual_form: Devices that disable ninja cat shapeshifting powers
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy advantage, environmental hazard to player faction abilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Bizarre amusement park setup on Awaji Island
danger_signals.meaning: Disruption of natural order, enemy territory control, world corruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Clockwork ninja army guarding Old Wise Man's palace
danger_signals.meaning: Dehumanized threat, mechanized power, non-negotiable opposition

safety_signals.visual_form: Kurobei (ninja cat revealed after defeating Ghost Woman), grey cats dropped by enemies, Koban clan representation
safety_signals.meaning: Allied faction, support available, progression marker

safety_signals.visual_form: Oedo hometown, festival towns, Yamato town, Iga palace
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for commerce, information gathering, temporary refuge

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs offering item purchases (bombs, weapons), mini-game operators
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability, economic function preserved, civilization intact

safety_signals.visual_form: Old Wise Man in Iga palace, Princess Yuki as rescue objective
safety_signals.meaning: Guidance available, legitimate authority, mission legitimacy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ninja cat companions, Judo attack symbols
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposed by devices that disable cat shapeshifting; recognize each other through animal companion summoning
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Towns under investigation, temples as investigation points, traditional Japanese architecture as faction home territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cat form, shapeshifting ability, grey cat power-ups
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghost Woman disguise initially; cats recognizable by their shapeshifting capacity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temple origin point, association with Shikoku Island, presence in multiple regions of Japan

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mime and clown appearance, amusement park aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hyotoko dancers (festival infiltrators), clown/mime soldiers, army leaders with distinctive appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Anachronistic amusement park on Awaji Island, recruited townspeople, HQ location, Princess Yuki kidnapping base

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Clockwork ninjas (dehumanized warrior units), leader Sasuke with mechanical traits
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Iga palace, Dragon Pond region, Izumo location (White Mirror sacred site)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Legitimate target of kidnapping rather than active faction; represents order to restore

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Towns with active commerce, festivals occurring (Hyotoko dancers), temples standing normally
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal activities, players can freely explore towns, purchase items, play mini-games

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ghost Woman at temple, ghost animals visible at night, suspicious Hyotoko dancers present in festivals, amusement park erected on island
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople express suspicion of dancers, ghost encounters trigger investigation, town exploration reveals NPC concerns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Otafu Army presence visible, anachronistic amusement park fully established, army has recruited townspeople into ranks, Yamato town shows clear occupation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Recruited townspeople assist enemy army, HQ is fortified and centralized, players must fight through recruited civilians

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shikoku Island shows devices disabling shapeshifting, Iga palace surrounded by robotic clockwork ninjas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ninja cats unable to investigate (movement restriction), players must approach without cat-specific advantages, boss Sasuke leads mechanical defense

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple false leads (Ghost Woman is actually Kurobei, Otafu Army didn't kidnap princess), White Mirror location requires quest across multiple regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players directed between locations, must gather clues from NPCs, authority figures (Koban, Old Wise Man) provide directional guidance

environmental_storytelling.example: Temple at Oedo emanates ghost animals at night, signaling supernatural breach and corruption of traditionally protective location
Amusement park on Awaji Island visually communicates that Otafu Army is foreign/disrupting natural order and has resources for major construction
Townspeople in festival towns express visible distrust of Hyotoko dancers, communicating player to observe these NPCs as infiltrators without explicit dialogue
Presence of shapeshifting-disabling devices communicates that Otafu Army specifically researched and countered ninja cat abilities
Townspeople actively recruited into Otafu Army indicates progression from infiltration to active occupation/control of settlements
Old Wise Man isolated in heavily defended Iga palace with mechanical guardians communicates his knowledge is powerful enough to warrant extreme protection
Princess Yuki kidnapping serves as narrative anchor for player mission; her status as imperial daughter communicates this is legitimate state-level threat, not petty crime

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Haunts temple, appears as ghost, attacks players investigating temple
Transforms to ninja cat form, offers payment and recruits player to larger mission, provides directional guidance
Participate in festival activities but trigger NPC suspicion; become hostiles when confronted
Leader of evil dancers faction, must be defeated to progress, his defeat rescues Koban
Actively recruit townspeople, set up fortifications on islands, operate amusement park as cover/front
Assist enemy faction, block player progression, must be fought through rather than negotiated with
Mechanical behavior (no negotiation possible), defend Iga palace perimeter, prevent access to Old Wise Man
Leads robotic defense, must be defeated to access Old Wise Man's chamber, guards passage to Dragon Pond information
Rescued by players, reveals Otafu Army's framing regarding princess, directs quest toward Iga investigation
Provides ultimate information (White Mirror location), uses 'Miracle Transport Machine' to advance player to final quest stage

notes: The Legend of the Mystical Ninja uses visual hierarchy and environmental design to communicate faction control and world corruption. The progression from peaceful town exploration → infiltration detection → active occupation → technological lockdown creates a world-state gradient visible through NPC reactions, environmental aesthetics (temple vs amusement park), and enemy composition shifts. Friendly faction (ninja cats) is visually distinct through animal form and grey power-up drops. Enemy faction (Otafu Army) is marked by anachronistic mime/clown aesthetics contrasting with traditional Japanese setting. The presence of authority figures (Old Wise Man, Princess Yuki) in heavily defended locations reinforces political legitimacy of player mission. Shapeshifting-disabling devices represent faction-specific counter-strategy visible in environment. Town commerce and mini-games serve as safety signals indicating civilian presence and economic function. Multi-stage structure (town exploration + platforming boss fight) allows visual communication of both political/social corruption and combat threat separately.
